top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 102, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Tour de Palm Springs' flat to gently rolling desert roads through the rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour de Palm Springs. Train it until it feels boring.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour de Palm Springs' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before February.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 102 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
102 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Tour de Palm Springs' flat to gently rolling desert roads through the actually does to you.
When someone attacks on flat to gently rolling desert roads through the, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 102 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tempo is Tour de Palm Springs' race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 102 miles on flat to gently rolling desert roads through the. Train it until it's automatic.
At Tour de Palm Springs, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Tour de Palm Springs' flat to gently rolling desert roads through the forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 102 miles.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour de Palm Springs’s 102 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and G Spot to prepare you for 102 miles of flat to gently rolling desert roads through the coachella valley in Palm Springs, California, USA.
